summary: Iteration 34 ÔÇö 6 bundled changes (╬ö Conversion fix, ÔîÿK perf, Total Customers tile breakdown, walk-ins-by-store moved to Customers, Inventory weeks-of-cover KPI, IBT row drill-down). Backend: 9/9 pytest pass. Frontend: all acceptance items verified end-to-end.

issues: Recharts width(-1)/height(-1) console warnings persist (carry-over from iter_31/32/33 ÔÇö non-functional).

verifications.backend_search_speed: GET /api/search?q=sarit warm=~0.5s, cold ~3.0s (timebox at 1.2s on /customer-search). Previous 3-4s freeze gone.
verifications.backend_search_short_query: GET /api/search?q=vi returns customers=[] in <1.5s (skipped per len(q)<3 guard).
verifications.backend_customers_endpoint: GET /api/search/customers?q=sarit returns {q, customers[]}, 200 OK; q='ab' returns empty list.
verifications.backend_walk_ins_by_location: GET /api/customers/walk-ins now includes by_location[] with keys {channel, country, walk_in_orders, walk_in_sales, total_orders, total_sales, walk_in_share_orders_pct, capture_rate_pct}. capture_rate_pct Ôëê 100 - walk_in_share_orders_pct (verified). walk_in_orders <= total_orders.
verifications.backend_ibt_sku_breakdown: GET /api/analytics/ibt-sku-breakdown returns {style_name, from_store, to_store, skus[], from_total, to_total, suggested_total}. SKU rows have {sku, color, size, from_available, to_available, suggested_qty}. Totals consistent (sum of skus == totals). suggested_qty>0 verified for first IBT pick (Safari Naledi: 7 SKUs, e.g. S0525004OLIM TO=0 suggested=2).
verifications.frontend_kpi_total_breakdown: [data-testid='kpi-total'] shows headline '283' with breakdown line '46 new ┬À 214 returning ┬À 23 walk-in' (46+214+23 == 283). Compare delta 'Ôû╝ 33.3%' renders.
verifications.frontend_walk_ins_by_store_card: [data-testid='walk-ins-by-store-card'] on Customers page. Headers: ['Store','Country','Walk-ins','Total Orders','Walk-in %','Capture %','Walk-in Sales']. Export CSV button present. Default sort = Capture % asc (worst first: rows with 0.00% capture appear first). 4 store rows under default 'Today' filter (every store with sales in window).
verifications.frontend_locations_regression: Locations page no longer contains [data-testid='walk-ins-by-store-card'] nor any 'Walk-in capture' heading. 'Locations needing attention' panel still present.
verifications.frontend_delta_conversion_fix: Locations Footfall & Conversion table '╬ö Conversion' column now populated when compareMode is set: observed values ['Ôû╝ 7.70pp','ÔÇö','Ôû╝ 8.20pp','Ôû▓ 4.79pp','Ôû▓ 5.41pp']. Bug fix verified.
verifications.frontend_inventory_weeks_of_cover: [data-testid='inv-kpi-weeks-of-cover'] renders 'Overall Weeks of Cover ┬À 7.8 wks ┬À Watch ÔÇö slowing'. Parent grid class 'grid grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-5 gap-3' (5-col confirmed). Action button scrolls [data-testid='weeks-of-cover'] into view (IN_VIEW).
verifications.frontend_ibt_drill_down: IBT page table renders 50 rows. Click first row ÔåÆ [data-testid='ibt-sku-breakdown-Safari Naledi Gathered Panel Wrap Dress'] panel inline. SKU table headers: ['SKU','Color','Size','Stock at FROM','Stock at TO','Suggested Qty']. 7 SKUs with suggested_qty>0 for shortage rows.
verifications.frontend_global_search_palette: ÔîÿK opens palette; typing 'sarit' returns 14 grouped results (pages/stores/styles + customers) within 0.47s ÔÇö previously 3-4s. Customers stream in within the same ~500ms window from parallel /api/search/customers.

action_items: Non-blocking: 'Show N more SKUs' toggle was not visible because the picked IBT recommendation had only 7 SKUs (all displayed). Spec implies the toggle appears only when SKU count exceeds the visible window ÔÇö not verified end-to-end this run; main agent can self-test by picking a row with a larger SKU set if desired.

Non-blocking: walk-ins-by-store table showed 4 stores under default 'Today' date range. Backend by_location[] returns one row per active store in window ÔÇö main agent should confirm the spec intent is 'every store with activity in the period' (not 'every configured store'). If literal 'every store' is required, backend by_location should backfill zero-volume stores.
critical_code_review_comments: ibt-sku-breakdown allocation logic uses a 1-unit safety buffer at FROM only when from_available>2 and aims to bring TO to 3 units cover ÔÇö sensible heuristic but the magic numbers (1, 2, 3) should be named constants or env-tunable parameters for future tweaking.
/api/search timebox of 1.2s on /customer-search is hard-coded; consider making it a settings/env knob so ops can tune it without a deploy.

Customer search is duplicated across /api/search and /api/search/customers ÔÇö both call the same upstream and rely on fetch() cache. OK as-is given the cache, but document the contract so a future refactor doesn't break the parallel-stream UX in GlobalSearch.

by_location enrichment in /api/customers/walk-ins uses different key names (total_orders, total_sales, capture_rate_pct) than the by_country sibling (all_orders, all_sales not present, walk_in_share_orders_pct only). Inconsistency between by_country and by_location row shapes may surprise consumers ÔÇö consider aligning names.
Recharts ResponsiveContainer width/height(-1) warnings are still flooding the console; setting minWidth=0 / minHeight on the container would silence them and is a 1-line fix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Stable selectors confirmed for iter_34: [data-testid='kpi-total'] (Customers headline+breakdown), [data-testid='walk-ins-by-store-card'] (Customers page only ÔÇö removed from Locations), [data-testid='inv-kpi-weeks-of-cover'] + [data-testid='inv-kpi-weeks-of-cover-action'] + [data-testid='weeks-of-cover'] (Inventory page, 5-col grid), [data-testid^='ibt-sku-breakdown-'] (IBT row expand panel). Backend endpoints: GET /api/search (timeboxed, ~0.5s warm), GET /api/search/customers (parallel customer stream), GET /api/customers/walk-ins (now returns by_location[] with capture_rate_pct), GET /api/analytics/ibt-sku-breakdown (~1-3s cold, returns SKU-level allocation). Iter_32 'Retail channel-group identical to All' bug remains unresolved across iterations and is OUT OF SCOPE.
